---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 1dcc3de6dc12a568400be6ed3c5e1fcc3ed18ace
findings:
  - { id: judged-j-1, kind: judged, text: "ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a value-preserving full re-render (align.RenderMarkdown re-emits the ENTIRE frontmatter from the decoded struct) rather than a byte-preserving splice: for any report the sanctioned `verdi align` renderer produced, the round-trip is byte-identical and both verifiers (ComputeDigest recompute, VerifyIntegrity) provably still pass — the AC's own operationalization — but a living report whose frontmatter is valid under strict decode yet formatted differently (hand-authored per the retired round-6 flow, or written by an older renderer) would have every other finding's entry and the digest:/integrity:/judge_integrity: lines silently reformatted on write, byte-different though value-identical. The body-side desync check (exit-1 verdict) guards only the target finding's rendered line, not frontmatter formatting. Since ac-1 defines invisibility in terms of the two verifiers, which hold unconditionally, this is at most a divergence from the literal 'byte-for-byte' phrasing on inputs outside the sanctioned production path, not a verifier-visible one. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-50 (2026-07-16), restated through ADJ-52/53/54's later sweeps: accepted-deviation. Value-preserving canonical re-rendering (decode -\u003e value-copy -\u003e align.RenderMarkdown) IS the store's own determinism discipline, not a departure from it; the AC's two operative verifier claims (stored digest matches an independent recomputation; VerifyIntegrity succeeds against the persisted judge exchange) hold unconditionally, and every report this verb operates on in practice is itself align-generated and already canonical. The verb fails closed on any detectable body/frontmatter mismatch (ADJ-53 j-5) rather than silently reformatting; a hypothetical noncanonical-but-valid frontmatter converging to canonical form on write is convergence, not loss." }

The body-side desync check (exit-1 verdict) guards only the target finding's rendered line, not frontmatter formatting. Since ac-1 defines invisibility in terms of the two verifiers, which hold unconditionally, this is at most a divergence from the literal 'byte-for-byte' phrasing on inputs outside the sanctioned production path, not a verifier-visible one.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}" }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-j-1** [accepted-deviation]: ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a value-preserving full re-render (align.RenderMarkdown re-emits the ENTIRE frontmatter from the decoded struct) rather than a byte-preserving splice: for any report the sanctioned `verdi align` renderer produced, the round-trip is byte-identical and both verifiers (ComputeDigest recompute, VerifyIntegrity) provably still pass — the AC's own operationalization — but a living report whose frontmatter is valid under strict decode yet formatted differently (hand-authored per the retired round-6 flow, or written by an older renderer) would have every other finding's entry and the digest:/integrity:/judge_integrity: lines silently reformatted on write, byte-different though value-identical. The body-side desync check (exit-1 verdict) guards only the target finding's rendered line, not frontmatter formatting. Since ac-1 defines invisibility in terms of the two verifiers, which hold unconditionally, this is at most a divergence from the literal 'byte-for-byte' phrasing on inputs outside the sanctioned production path, not a verifier-visible one. (confidence 0.20) — Controller adjudication ADJ-50 (2026-07-16), restated through ADJ-52/53/54's later sweeps: accepted-deviation. Value-preserving canonical re-rendering (decode -> value-copy -> align.RenderMarkdown) IS the store's own determinism discipline, not a departure from it; the AC's two operative verifier claims (stored digest matches an independent recomputation; VerifyIntegrity succeeds against the persisted judge exchange) hold unconditionally, and every report this verb operates on in practice is itself align-generated and already canonical. The verb fails closed on any detectable body/frontmatter mismatch (ADJ-53 j-5) rather than silently reformatting; a hypothetical noncanonical-but-valid frontmatter converging to canonical form on write is convergence, not loss.
